questions.question: The author compares God's promise to Abraham to what unbreakable thing?
options: An oath confirmed by two unchangeable things
questions.correct: An oath confirmed by two unchangeable things
questions.feedback: God confirmed his promise with an oath — two unchangeable things in which God cannot lie.

questions.question: What does the author call hope?
questions.feedback: Hope is an anchor for the soul, firm and secure.

questions.question: The author says it is possible to restore to repentance those who have fallen away after being enlightened.
questions.feedback: The author says it is impossible to restore them to repentance if they fall away.

questions.question: The author urges them to move beyond elementary teachings and press on to _____.
questions.feedback: Leave the elementary doctrine and go on to maturity.

questions.question: God is not unjust; he will not forget their work and the _____ they have shown him.
questions.feedback: God will not forget their work and the love they showed in his name.
